preview_text:
The model favors France heavily, assigning them a 80% win probability. Sweden's chance sits at just 2%, leaving the underdog prospect minimal in this matchup on a neutral ground.

The forecast shows a pronounced asymmetry in the win-loss split, with France commanding the bulk of the probability mass. A draw remains plausible at 18%, suggesting that while France are clear favorites, the match retains some competitive shape rather than appearing a foregone conclusion. The 77-point gap between France's win probability and Sweden's reflects a substantial quality differential according to the model.

The model and sportsbook consensus diverge meaningfully on Sweden's chances. The model gives Sweden 2%, while the market consensus across 48 books reaches 9%—a 7-percentage-point gap. France's win probability aligns more closely (model 80%, market 75%), and draws sit within 2 points. This dashboard does not adjudicate which estimate better reflects underlying match conditions.
